title: Matthiola viscosa
taxonomy: Matthiola viscosa belongs to the Brassicaceae family of plants, which includes a wide variety of species commonly known as mustards or cabbages. It is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. The genus Matthiola is often referred to as the stock or evening stock, and it comprises several species of annual or short-lived perennials. The classification of Matthiola viscosa is further detailed with its binomial nomenclature, which is used universally in botany to identify and classify plants. This system of classification was developed by Carl Linnaeus, a Swedish botanist who is often considered the father of taxonomy.

intro: Matthiola viscosa, also known as sticky stock or sticky stock gillyflower, is a perennial plant in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its sticky stems and fragrant, purple flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Matthiola viscosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed to treat respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and coughs.
